# [enhancement] ORPHAN_CONCEPT signal — distinct display channel in sandman_handoff.md

## Context

PR [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084) (resolves #10035) detects orphan concepts (CONCEPT nodes with no `IMPLEMENTED_BY` edge — concepts that exist in the ontology but aren't anchored to any source code) and emits them as `logger.warn` during ingestion. The choice to suppress them from the capability-gap channel was deliberate: orphans are ontology data-quality signals, not coverage gaps, and conflating them would dilute the signal `GoldenPathSynthesizer` consumes for `sandman_handoff.md`.

As the ontology grows (#10050 description enrichment, #10036 Memory Core concept discovery, #10037 ChromaDB embedding), orphans may become frequent enough that *invisibility* becomes the wrong default. A dedicated display channel is the right fix — keeps the main gap channel clean while making orphans actionable for curators.

## The Fix

1. `ConceptIngestor` emits `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` entries to a new property channel (e.g., `node.properties.dataQualityFlag` to keep it separate from `capabilityGap`) OR to the same `capabilityGap` JSON array with the distinct tag — decide during implementation based on which is cleaner downstream
2. `GoldenPathSynthesizer` categorizes `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` into a new array parallel to `testGaps` / `guideGaps` / `exampleGaps`
3. New rendering section in `sandman_handoff.md`: `### ⚠️ Orphaned Concepts (X of Y items)` with the same 5-item limit pattern
4. TTL pruning applies uniformly

## Decision Needed During Implementation

Should `ORPHAN_CONCEPT` share the `capabilityGap` property channel with `TEST_GAP`/`GUIDE_GAP`/`EXAMPLE_GAP`, or live on its own property? Sharing reduces GoldenPathSynthesizer's node-scanning work (one loop not two); separating keeps the "coverage gap" semantics pure. Lean: share the channel, since the tag-prefix categorization pattern already handles taxonomy separation downstream.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ConceptIngestor attaches `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` entries to orphan nodes
- [ ] GoldenPathSynthesizer categorizes the new tag + renders dedicated section
- [ ] `logger.warn` simplified — warn only on first detection per orphan (TTL-based dedup) or removed entirely if graph persistence is enough signal
- [ ] Existing tests still pass
- [ ] New test: ingesting an orphan concept causes `⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section to appear in handoff

## Origin Session ID

`62d6f155-e57f-4279-9b59-36c9e4ecbc5e` — flagged as out-of-scope in PR [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084) body

## Timeline

- 2026-04-19T10:31:51Z @tobiu added the `enhancement` label
- 2026-04-19T10:31:51Z @tobiu added the `ai` label
- 2026-04-19T10:32:35Z @tobiu added parent issue #10030
- 2026-04-19T10:33:36Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10030
- 2026-04-19T14:16:11Z @tobiu referenced in commit `6f0263f` - "refactor(ai): hoist concept-graph gap pass to cycle-scope (#10085)

Items 1 + 2 of #10085 from #10084's self-review.

- Item 1: hoisted `inferConceptGraphGaps` from per-session to cycle-scope;
  deleted the `executeCapabilityGapInference` wrapper; promoted
  `inferTestGapsFromSession` and `inferConceptGraphGaps` to public entry
  points on both `GapInferenceEngine` and the thin DreamService facade.
  The cycle-scope call now fires once in `processUndigestedSessions` after
  the per-session loop, before `runGarbageCollection`.
- Item 2: new `test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/ConceptIngestor.spec.mjs`
  with six coverage targets: differential-sync skip path, differential-sync
  re-upsert on hash mismatch, orphan detection + logger.warn emission,
  canonical CONCEPT_EDGE_TYPES filtering, stub-node seeding by namespace
  (file:/ext:/concept), idempotency, stats return-shape contract.

Items 3 + 4 dropped after scope review:

- Item 3 (log `syncConceptsToGraph` stats via `logger.info` in DreamService):
  redundant. `ConceptIngestor.syncConceptsToGraph` already emits the phase
  summary at completion. DreamService runs offline; logger output is
  ephemeral; no durable observability value in re-emission.
- Item 4 (consolidate per-orphan `logger.warn`): superseded by #10087, which
  defines the correct architecture (capabilityGap tag channel with a new
  [ORPHAN_CONCEPT] tag, weight-gated via GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD, surfaced
  through a dedicated sandman_handoff.md section). Logger-level consolidation
  would land the wrong fix in the wrong ticket. Per-orphan warn preserved
  here; deletion happens in the #10087 PR that introduces the handoff channel.

37/37 tests pass: ConceptService 23, DreamService 6, DreamServiceGoldenPath 1,
ConceptIngestor 7 new. Stable across 3 consecutive runs.

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326

* test(ai): strengthen ConceptIngestor spec + hoist-behavioral test (#10085)

Addresses the Required Actions from the #10100 self-review:

- ConceptIngestor.spec.mjs: strengthened idempotency assertion — second
  sync must hit the hash-match skip path (`conceptsSkipped > 0`,
  `conceptsUpserted === 0`, `edgesReplaced === 0`) in addition to the
  graph-state equivalence check. A differential-sync regression that
  silently re-upserted on the second call would have previously passed.

- ConceptIngestor.spec.mjs: symmetric afterEach now resets
  `ConceptService.loaded = false` (and clears the node/edge/alias indexes)
  so the cross-spec singleton doesn't leak `loaded = true` into
  ConceptService.spec.mjs:138 ("should throw if query methods are called
  before loadGraph") when `fullyParallel: true` interleaves tests in the
  same worker. This closes a pre-existing intermittent flake sensitive to
  Playwright scheduling; 8/8 consecutive full-suite runs pass.

- DreamService.spec.mjs: new behavioral test for the Item 1 hoist contract
  — stubs the REM pipeline surface, runs processUndigestedSessions with
  N=3 mock sessions, asserts inferTestGapsFromSession called N times and
  inferConceptGraphGaps called exactly once AFTER the last session update.
  Locks in the efficiency claim with a regression guard so future
  "simplifications" that re-inline the cycle-scope call into the session
  loop fail fast.

All stubs restored via try/finally.

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T14:47:41Z @tobiu assigned to @tobiu
- 2026-04-19T14:58:36Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b91d523` - "feat(ai): ORPHAN_CONCEPT signal + ⚠️ Orphaned Concepts handoff section (#10087)

Promotes orphan concept detection from the ephemeral per-orphan `logger.warn`
in `ConceptIngestor` to the durable `capabilityGap` tag channel + dedicated
`sandman_handoff.md` section — same pattern as `[GUIDE_GAP]` / `[EXAMPLE_GAP]`
established in #10084. Surfaces ontology data-quality issues where humans and
future agents can act on them rather than scroll past in daemon logs.

## Architecture

- `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps` now emits a third weight-gated
  signal alongside `[GUIDE_GAP]` / `[EXAMPLE_GAP]`: `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` when a
  concept has no outbound `IMPLEMENTED_BY` edge. Orthogonal to the other two
  — a concept can be both orphaned and lack a guide. Gates via the shared
  `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD` so today's guide-title-derived ingestion (thin,
  mostly low-weight concepts) stays silent; the signal auto-surfaces as richer
  ingestion lands (#10036 / #10037 / #10050) without config changes.
- Action-biased entry text: "Concept X has no IMPLEMENTED_BY edge — either
  anchor it to a source file or retire the concept from nodes.jsonl if
  aspirational/stale."
- `ConceptIngestor.syncConceptsToGraph` deletes the per-orphan `logger.warn`
  (one line per orphan × N cycles = log spam growing linearly with ontology
  size). Retains `stats.orphansDetected` for the cycle-summary info line —
  which is the appropriate place for phase-level observability.
- `GoldenPathSynthesizer` parses the new `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` tag and renders
  a `### ⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section with the same 5-item limit + TTL
  pruning as the other tag channels.

## Tests (39/39 stable × 5 runs)

- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: new test for three orphan-detection cases —
  fully-wired concept (no gaps), EXPLAINED_BY + EXEMPLIFIED_BY but no
  IMPLEMENTED_BY (ORPHAN_CONCEPT only, no GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP), and
  low-weight orphan (weight gate blocks emission).
- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: `synthesizeGoldenPath` test extended — planted a
  CONCEPT node with pre-written `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` capabilityGap; asserts
  the final handoff content includes the `⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section.
- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: GUIDE_GAP covered-concept fixture now includes an
  IMPLEMENTED_BY edge (otherwise the fully-covered control concept would
  correctly emit the new ORPHAN_CONCEPT signal, inverting the test's premise).
- `ConceptIngestor.spec.mjs`: orphan test asserts `stats.orphansDetected`
  remains accurate AND no per-orphan logger.warn fires (the AC3 deletion).

## AC status

- [x] ConceptIngestor attaches orphan signal (now via GapInferenceEngine → capabilityGap) — the architectural home for concept-gap emission
- [x] GoldenPathSynthesizer categorizes `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` + renders dedicated section
- [x] logger.warn removed entirely (graph persistence is the durable signal)
- [x] Existing tests still pass — 39/39 stable
- [x] New test: ingesting an orphan concept causes `⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section to appear in handoff

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T14:59:29Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10101
- 2026-04-19T15:11:02Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b00125b` - "test(ai): co-emission spec + symmetric DreamService afterEach (#10087)

Addresses the optional follow-ups from #10101's self-review:

- New test for GUIDE_GAP + ORPHAN_CONCEPT co-emission on a concept lacking
  BOTH EXPLAINED_BY and IMPLEMENTED_BY. Locks in the branch orthogonality —
  the GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP pair is mutually exclusive, but ORPHAN_CONCEPT
  is independent. The three-case test (fully-wired / orphan-only / low-weight)
  didn't hit the compound case directly; this does.

- Symmetric `afterEach` in DreamService.spec.mjs mirroring the existing
  `beforeEach` graph-state cleanup. Applies the `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md`
  discipline established in #10100: `fullyParallel: true` interleaves specs
  in the same worker, and `GraphService.db` is a shared singleton. Without
  `afterEach`, the last test's fixtures (CONCEPT nodes planted by the
  ORPHAN_CONCEPT detection tests) leak to whatever test Playwright queues
  next from another spec. The existing `afterAll` nullifies `GraphService.db`
  so cross-spec leak is bounded at worker-shutdown, but per-test symmetric
  cleanup closes the door at the test-boundary level — belt-and-suspenders,
  aligned with the discipline I just codified.

40/40 tests pass across 5 consecutive runs.

## Tests (39/39 stable × 5 runs)

- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: new test for three orphan-detection cases —
  fully-wired concept (no gaps), EXPLAINED_BY + EXEMPLIFIED_BY but no

- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: GUIDE_GAP covered-concept fixture now includes an

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T15:19:00Z @tobiu referenced in commit `db5bdae` - "feat(ai): config-lift guideGapWeightThreshold to aiConfig.data (#10086)

Promotes the previously file-local `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 0.8` constant
in `GapInferenceEngine` to `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold`, making the
weight gate curator-tunable without a code change. Default value preserved
(`0.8`); the derivation comment moves from the const JSDoc to the config
template next to the value itself, where tuners will see it.

## Scope

- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`: adds `guideGapWeightThreshold:
  Number(process.env.NEO_GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD) || 0.8` after `decayFactor`
  (both graph-pipeline behavior floats — semantic grouping). JSDoc carries the
  full derivation from ConceptService.calculateWeight plus the post-#10087
  context (threshold now gates three signals, not just GUIDE_GAP — historical
  prefix retained per ticket AC).
- `ai/daemons/services/GapInferenceEngine.mjs`: removes the file-local const,
  imports `Memory_Config as aiConfig` via the services SDK, caches the
  threshold once per cycle at the top of `inferConceptGraphGaps` so the live
  config value is consulted (tests can mutate it; module-load capture would
  miss runtime overrides). Class + method JSDoc updated to reference the
  config path and explain the historical `guideGap*` prefix (introduced in
  #10035 for GUIDE_GAP alone; widened in #10087 to gate all three concept-
  graph signals — rename considered but scoped out to keep this PR tight).

**Parity note:** the gitignored local `config.mjs` must mirror the template
addition for tests to pick up the key. Updated locally in this worktree but
not committed (by design — the template is the source of truth for the
shipped repo; each checkout's `config.mjs` is derived manually or via
`bootstrapWorktree.mjs` on worktree creation).

## Tests (41/41 stable × 5 runs)

New test `threshold override via aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold
changes emission behavior (#10086)`:
- Plants a tier-2 concept with weight 0.5 (below default 0.8 threshold).
- Overrides `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold = 0.3` inside try/finally.
- Asserts the concept now emits both `[GUIDE_GAP]` and `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]`,
  proving the config is read at gate time (not captured at module load).
- Restores the original value in `finally`.

The existing "skip low-weight concepts" test (weight 0.3 with default 0.8)
remains and now acts as the counterpart — default config path.

## AC checklist

- [x] `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` exists in `config.template.mjs`
      with default 0.8 + derivation comment (moved from GapInferenceEngine)
- [x] `GapInferenceEngine` reads the value from config, not from a file-local
      constant — verified via runtime-override test
- [x] Existing tests pass (40 → 41 total, all green × 5 consecutive runs)
- [x] New test proves threshold override via config produces different
      gap-emission behavior

## Related

- Parent epic: #10030
- Origin PR: #10084 (introduced the const)
- Preceded by: #10100 (scope hoist), #10101 (ORPHAN_CONCEPT — now also gated
  by this threshold, so config-lift value compounded)
- Weight formula precedent: `ConceptService.calculateWeight` (tier + uniqueness + coverage_deficit)

Resolves #10086

      constant — verified via runtime-override test
- [x] Existing tests pass (40 → 41 total, all green × 5 consecutive runs)

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T15:39:36Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10104
- 2026-04-19T16:05:15Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10105
